<!--
  @component User settings wrapper

  Properties:
  - `username String!`: Empty string if auth. is disabled, otherwise the username as string
  - `isApi Bool!`: Is currently logged in user api authority
-->

<script>
  import { getContext } from "svelte";
  import UserOptions from "./user/UserOptions.svelte";
  import PlotRenderOptions from "./user/PlotRenderOptions.svelte";
  import PlotColorScheme from "./user/PlotColorScheme.svelte";

  /* Svelte 5 Props */
  let {
    username,
    isApi
  } = $props();

  /* Const Init */
  const ccconfig = getContext("cc-config");
  
  /* State Init */
  let message = $state({ msg: "", target: "", color: "#d63384" });
  let displayMessage = $state(false);
  let cbmode = $state(ccconfig?.plotConfiguration_colorblindMode || false);

  /* Functions */
  async function handleSettingSubmit(event, setting) {
    event.preventDefault();

    const selector = setting.selector
    const target = setting.target
    let form = document.querySelector(selector);
    let formData = new FormData(form);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(form.action, { method: "POST", body: formData });
      if (res.ok) {
        let text = await res.text();
        if (formData.get("key") === "plotConfiguration_colorblindMode") {
          cbmode = JSON.parse(formData.get("value"));
        }
        popMessage(text, target, "#048109");
      } else {
        let text = await res.text();
        throw new Error("Response Code " + res.status + "-> " + text);
      }
    } catch (err) {
      popMessage(err, target, "#d63384");
    }

    return false;
  }

  function popMessage(response, restarget, rescolor) {
    message = { msg: response, target: restarget, color: rescolor };
    displayMessage = true;
    setTimeout(function () {
      displayMessage = false;
    }, 3500);
  }
</script>

<UserOptions config={ccconfig} {username} {isApi} bind:message bind:displayMessage updateSetting={(e, newSetting) => handleSettingSubmit(e, newSetting)}/>
<PlotRenderOptions config={ccconfig} bind:message bind:displayMessage updateSetting={(e, newSetting) => handleSettingSubmit(e, newSetting)}/>
<PlotColorScheme config={ccconfig} bind:cbmode bind:message bind:displayMessage updateSetting={(e, newSetting) => handleSettingSubmit(e, newSetting)}/>

<!--
  @component Admin settings wrapper

  Properties:
  - `ncontent String`: The homepage notice content
  - `clusterNames [String]`: The available clusternames
-->

<script>
  import { Row, Col } from "@sveltestrap/sveltestrap";
  import { onMount, getContext } from "svelte";
  import EditRole from "./admin/EditRole.svelte";
  import EditProject from "./admin/EditProject.svelte";
  import AddUser from "./admin/AddUser.svelte";
  import ShowUsers from "./admin/ShowUsers.svelte";
  import Options from "./admin/Options.svelte";
  import NoticeEdit from "./admin/NoticeEdit.svelte";
  import RunTaggers from "./admin/RunTaggers.svelte";

  /* Svelte 5 Props */
  let {
    ncontent,
    clusterNames
  } = $props();

  /* Const Init*/
  const ccconfig = getContext("cc-config");

  /* State Init */
  let users = $state([]);
  let roles = $state([]);

  /* Functions */
  function getUserList() {
    fetch("/config/users/?via-ldap=false&not-just-user=true")
      .then((res) => res.json())
      .then((usersRaw) => {
        users = usersRaw;
      });
  }

  function getValidRoles() {
    fetch("/config/roles/")
      .then((res) => res.json())
      .then((rolesRaw) => {
        roles = rolesRaw;
      });
  }

  function initAdmin() {
    getUserList();
    getValidRoles();
  }

  /* on Mount */
  onMount(() => initAdmin());
</script>

<Row cols={2} class="p-2 g-2">
  <Col class="mb-1">
    <AddUser {roles} reloadUser={() => getUserList()} />
  </Col>
  <Col class="mb-1">
    <ShowUsers reloadUser={() => getUserList()} bind:users />
  </Col>
  <Col>
    <EditRole {roles} reloadUser={() => getUserList()} />
  </Col>
  <Col>
    <EditProject reloadUser={() => getUserList()} />
  </Col>
  <Options config={ccconfig} {clusterNames}/>
  <NoticeEdit {ncontent}/>
  <RunTaggers />
</Row>

<!--
  @component Main Config Option Component, Wrapper for admin and user sub-components

  Properties:
  - `ìsAdmin Bool!`: Is currently logged in user admin authority
  - `isSupport Bool!`: Is currently logged in user support authority
  - `isApi Bool!`: Is currently logged in user api authority
  - `username String!`: Empty string if auth. is disabled, otherwise the username as string
  - `ncontent String!`: The currently displayed message on the homescreen
  - `clusterNames [String]`: The available clusternames
-->

<script>
  import { Card, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@sveltestrap/sveltestrap";
  import UserSettings from "./config/UserSettings.svelte";
  import SupportSettings from "./config/SupportSettings.svelte";
  import AdminSettings from "./config/AdminSettings.svelte";

  /* Svelte 5 Props */
  let {
    isAdmin,
    isSupport,
    isApi,
    username,
    ncontent,
    clusterNames
  } = $props();
</script>

{#if isAdmin}
  <Card style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle class="mb-1">Admin Options</CardTitle>
    </CardHeader>
    <AdminSettings {ncontent} {clusterNames}/>
  </Card>
{/if}

{#if isSupport || isAdmin}
  <Card style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle class="mb-1">Support Options</CardTitle>
    </CardHeader>
    <SupportSettings/>
  </Card>
{/if}

<Card>
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle class="mb-1">User Options</CardTitle>
  </CardHeader>
  <UserSettings {username} {isApi}/>
</Card>

<!--
  @component Admin settings wrapper

  Properties:
  - `ncontent String`: The homepage notice content
  - `clusterNames [String]`: The available clusternames
-->

<script>
  import { Row, Col } from "@sveltestrap/sveltestrap";
  import { onMount, getContext } from "svelte";
  import { fade } from "svelte/transition";
  import EditRole from "./admin/EditRole.svelte";
  import EditProject from "./admin/EditProject.svelte";
  import AddUser from "./admin/AddUser.svelte";
  import ShowUsers from "./admin/ShowUsers.svelte";
  import Options from "./admin/Options.svelte";
  import NoticeEdit from "./admin/NoticeEdit.svelte";
  import RunTaggers from "./admin/RunTaggers.svelte";
  import PlotRenderOptions from "./user/PlotRenderOptions.svelte";

  /* Svelte 5 Props */
  let {
    ncontent,
    clusterNames
  } = $props();

  /* Const Init*/
  const ccconfig = getContext("cc-config");

  /* State Init */
  let users = $state([]);
  let roles = $state([]);
  let message = $state({ msg: "", target: "", color: "#d63384" });
  let displayMessage = $state(false);

  /* Functions */
  function getUserList() {
    fetch("/config/users/?via-ldap=false&not-just-user=true")
      .then((res) => res.json())
      .then((usersRaw) => {
        users = usersRaw;
      });
  }

  function getValidRoles() {
    fetch("/config/roles/")
      .then((res) => res.json())
      .then((rolesRaw) => {
        roles = rolesRaw;
      });
  }

  function initAdmin() {
    getUserList();
    getValidRoles();
  }

  async function handleSettingSubmit(event, setting) {
    event.preventDefault();

    const selector = setting.selector
    const target = setting.target
    let form = document.querySelector(selector);
    let formData = new FormData(form);
    try {
      const res = await fetch(form.action, { method: "POST", body: formData });
      if (res.ok) {
        let text = await res.text();
        popMessage(text, target, "#048109");
      } else {
        let text = await res.text();
        throw new Error("Response Code " + res.status + "-> " + text);
      }
    } catch (err) {
      popMessage(err, target, "#d63384");
    }

    return false;
  }

  function popMessage(response, restarget, rescolor) {
    message = { msg: response, target: restarget, color: rescolor };
    displayMessage = true;
    setTimeout(function () {
      displayMessage = false;
    }, 3500);
  }

  /* on Mount */
  onMount(() => initAdmin());
</script>

<Row cols={2} class="p-2 g-2">
  <Col class="mb-1">
    <AddUser {roles} reloadUser={() => getUserList()} />
  </Col>
  <Col class="mb-1">
    <ShowUsers reloadUser={() => getUserList()} bind:users />
  </Col>
  <Col>
    <EditRole {roles} reloadUser={() => getUserList()} />
  </Col>
  <Col>
    <EditProject reloadUser={() => getUserList()} />
  </Col>
  <Options config={ccconfig} {clusterNames}/>
  <NoticeEdit {ncontent}/>
  <RunTaggers />
</Row>
<PlotRenderOptions config={ccconfig} bind:message bind:displayMessage updateSetting={(e, newSetting) => handleSettingSubmit(e, newSetting)}/>

     7→import "math"
     8→
     9→type ResamplePolicy string
    10→
    11→const (
    12→	ResamplePolicyLow    ResamplePolicy = "low"
    13→	ResamplePolicyMedium ResamplePolicy = "medium"
    14→	ResamplePolicyHigh   ResamplePolicy = "high"
    15→)
    16→
    17→// TargetPointsForPolicy returns the target number of data points for a given policy.
    18→func TargetPointsForPolicy(policy ResamplePolicy) int {
    19→	switch policy {
    20→	case ResamplePolicyLow:
    21→		return 200
    22→	case ResamplePolicyMedium:
    23→		return 500
    24→	case ResamplePolicyHigh:
    25→		return 1000
    26→	default:
    27→		return 0
    28→	}
    29→}
    30→
    31→// ComputeResolution computes the resampling resolution in seconds for a given
    32→// job duration, metric frequency, and target point count. Returns 0 if the
    33→// total number of data points is already at or below targetPoints (no resampling needed).
    34→func ComputeResolution(duration int64, frequency int64, targetPoints int) int {
    35→	if frequency <= 0 || targetPoints <= 0 || duration <= 0 {
    36→		return 0
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	totalPoints := duration / frequency
    40→	if totalPoints <= int64(targetPoints) {
    41→		return 0
    42→	}
    43→
    44→	targetRes := math.Ceil(float64(duration) / float64(targetPoints))
    45→	// Round up to nearest multiple of frequency
    46→	resolution := int(math.Ceil(targetRes/float64(frequency))) * int(frequency)
    47→
    48→	return resolution
    49→}

## Comprehensive Exploration: Policy-Based Resampling Approach

I've traced through the complete flow of the policy-based resampling system. Here's what I found:

### 1. Configuration Structure (config.json → Go Code)

**File: `/Users/jan/prg/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/config/config.go`**

The `ResampleConfig` struct now includes a new field:
- `MinimumPoints`: int (minimum points before resampling triggers)
- `Resolutions`: []int (array of target resolutions in seconds, e.g., [600, 300, 60])
- `Trigger`: int (threshold for switching between resolution levels)
- **`TargetPoints`: int (NEW - policy-derived target point count, set dynamically, NOT from config.json)**

The config.json mapping example from `configs/config.json`:

# Replace file-based resampling config with policy-based approach

## Context

The codebase has two resampling mechanisms:
1. **Old (file-based)**: `config.json` defines explicit `resolutions` array and `trigger` count. The frontend uses these to pick from a fixed set of resolution steps on zoom.
2. **New (policy-based)**: Users choose a policy ("low"/"medium"/"high") mapping to target point counts (200/500/1000). Resolution is computed dynamically from `duration / targetPoints`. This is already fully implemented in the GraphQL resolvers and frontend MetricPlot zoom logic.

The problem: `config.json` still uses the old `resolutions`+`trigger` schema, and falls back to it when no user policy is set. We should replace this with a default policy+algorithm in config, so the whole system is consistently policy-based.

## Changes

### 1. `internal/config/config.go` — Update `ResampleConfig` struct
- Remove `Resolutions []int` and `Trigger int` fields
- Add `DefaultPolicy string` (`json:"default-policy"`) — default resample policy ("low", "medium", "high")
- Add `DefaultAlgo string` (`json:"default-algo"`) — default resample algorithm ("lttb", "average", "simple")
- Keep `MinimumPoints int` — still useful for the resampler library
- Keep `TargetPoints int` — used by policy-derived runtime config

### 2. `internal/config/schema.go` — Update JSON schema
- Replace `trigger`/`resolutions` required properties with `default-policy`
- Add `default-policy` (string, enum: ["low","medium","high"]) and `default-algo` (string, enum: ["lttb","average","simple"]) properties
- Remove `trigger` and `resolutions` properties
- No required fields (presence of `"resampling": {}` is enough to enable it)

### 3. `internal/routerConfig/routes.go` — Update `resamplingForUser()`
- When falling back to globalCfg (no user policy set), use `globalCfg.DefaultPolicy` to compute `TargetPoints` and `Trigger` dynamically, same as the user-policy path
- If `DefaultPolicy` is empty, use "medium" as default

### 4. `internal/graph/schema.resolvers.go` — Update fallback resolution logic
- In `JobMetrics()` and `NodeMetricsList()`: replace `slices.Max(config.Keys.EnableResampling.Resolutions)` fallback with policy-based computation using `config.Keys.EnableResampling.DefaultPolicy`
- Use `TargetPointsForPolicy()` + `ComputeResolution()` instead of picking from a resolutions array
- Also apply `DefaultAlgo` when no user algo preference is set

### 5. `internal/graph/resample.go` — Update `resolveResampleAlgo()`
- After checking user preference, fall back to `config.Keys.EnableResampling.DefaultAlgo` before returning ""

### 6. `config.json` (local dev) — Update to new format
```json
"resampling": {
  "default-policy": "medium",
  "default-algo": "lttb"
}
```

### 7. `configs/config.json` (example config) — Update to new format
Same as above, keeping `minimum-points` if desired.

### 8. Frontend: No changes needed
The frontend already handles both modes:
- Policy-based: uses `targetPoints` to compute resolution dynamically
- Array-based: uses `resolutions` array for fixed steps

Since `resamplingForUser()` already returns a `ResampleConfig` with `TargetPoints`+`Trigger` (no `Resolutions`), the frontend will use the policy path.

## Files to modify
1. `internal/config/config.go` (struct + Init)
2. `internal/config/schema.go` (JSON schema)
3. `internal/routerConfig/routes.go` (`resamplingForUser`)
4. `internal/graph/schema.resolvers.go` (resolver fallbacks)
5. `internal/graph/resample.go` (`resolveResampleAlgo` fallback)
6. `config.json` (local config)
7. `configs/config.json` (example config)

## Verification
1. `go build ./...` — must compile
2. `go test ./internal/metricdispatch/...` — resample policy tests pass
3. `go test ./internal/config/...` — if config tests exist
4. Start with `./cc-backend -server -dev` and verify `/config` page shows resampling options
5. Verify metric plots on job pages still support zoom/resampling
